The core of the property is a global statement: "This property holds for every DM_i", which translates to the G (Globally) operator encompassing the entire formula. The condition that triggers the property is "in the case of TC messages sent from DM_i to the Local Node component", which maps to the atomic proposition 'tc_msg_sent_dm_to_local_node'. This condition implies a sequence of two events: first, "this component must forward the message to the Multiple Interface Association Set component" (mapped to 'msg_forwarded_to_multiple_interface_association_set'), and second, "which in turn must perform an update" (mapped to 'update_performed_by_multiple_interface_association_set'). The word "must" indicates that these events are guaranteed to happen eventually, so an F (Finally) operator is used for both. The phrase "which in turn" signifies a sequential dependency: the update happens after the forwarding. This is effectively captured by nesting the 'F update_performed_by_multiple_interface_association_set' within the scope of the 'msg_forwarded_to_multiple_interface_association_set'. Thus, the sequence 'A implies eventually (B and eventually C)' translates to A -> F(B & F C). Combining all parts, the formula becomes G(tc_msg_sent_dm_to_local_node -> F(msg_forwarded_to_multiple_interface_association_set & F update_performed_by_multiple_interface_association_set)).
